input.background: A $z$ test is conducted with $90\%$ power and $\alpha=0.05$ (two-sided). The variable has a known standard deviation $\sigma=40$. The null hypothesis assumes $\mu=170$, and the alternative hypothesis assumes $\mu=190$, with the difference worth detecting $\Delta=\mu_0 - \mu_a=170-190=-20$.
input.question: What is the standard error of the mean for the sample size of 42?
